skill.name: tool-design
skill.description: Design clearer agent tools with practical guidance for schemas, descriptions, error handling, response formats, and tool set organization.
skill.summary: Design agent tools that are easier for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to select and use correctly.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak-cryptography patterns plus external-command and network indicators, but review found they are false positives from prose, markdown code fences, example strings, and benign Python templates. No evidence found of command execution, outbound network calls, credential access, malicious intent, or prompt injection attempts in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are markdown code fences, inline formatting, or Python example blocks. No shell invocation or command execution logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is documentation formatting and non-executable example content. The Python scripts reviewed do not import subprocess, os.system, or similar execution APIs.

low_findings.title: Static Network Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported network indicators refer to tool naming concepts, URL parameter naming, or documentation examples. No HTTP client import or outbound request logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are prose about agent tool interfaces, not network operations. scripts/description_generator.py imports only typing and re, and scripts/verify.py reads local skill files only.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The high-volume weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary words and documentation content, not cryptographic API usage. No weak hash or encryption implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files contain skill metadata, markdown guidance, and template generation helpers. Review found no hashlib, crypto library, password storage, or encryption logic.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to ordinary documentation text, naming examples, or local file validation. No system inventory or host probing behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched locations are benign examples and prose. scripts/verify.py only checks for SKILL.md and required section names within the skill directory.

content.value_statement: Agents fail when tool names, inputs, outputs, or errors are ambiguous. This skill gives practical patterns for tool descriptions, schemas, response formats, and testing.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to write tool descriptions that define purpose, usage triggers, inputs, outputs, and errors.
Provides guidance for consolidating overlapping tools into clearer workflow-oriented tools.
Describes response format options that balance token efficiency with complete context.
Gives patterns for actionable error messages that help agents recover from failed calls.
Includes examples of strong and weak tool designs for comparison.
Provides a Python helper module for generating and evaluating tool description structures.
limitations: Does not implement production tool servers or integrations by itself.
Does not replace user testing with real agent workflows.
Examples are illustrative and may need adaptation for each framework.
Does not provide security hardening guidance for tool runtime permissions.

use_cases.title: Design a New Agent Tool
use_cases.description: Create a clear tool contract before exposing a workflow to an AI agent. Use the guidance to define inputs, outputs, errors, and usage triggers.

use_cases.title: Reduce Tool Selection Errors
use_cases.description: Review an existing tool set for overlap, vague descriptions, and inconsistent naming. Consolidate workflows when agents cannot choose reliably.

use_cases.title: Standardize MCP Tool Documentation
use_cases.description: Apply consistent namespacing and description patterns across Model Context Protocol tools. Make tool names easier for agents to reference correctly.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Tool
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single tool description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tool-design skill to review this tool description. Identify unclear purpose, missing inputs, missing outputs, and weak error guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Tool Contract
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate rewrite of a tool specification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tool-design skill to rewrite this tool contract. Include when to use it, parameter guidance, return shape, errors, and concise examples.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Tool Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of multiple related tools.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tool-design skill to evaluate this tool set for overlap, naming inconsistency, selection ambiguity, and opportunities to consolidate workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Testing Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced validation of agent tool behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tool-design skill to create an agent testing plan for these tools. Include representative requests, expected tool calls, failure modes, and refinement steps.

output_examples.input: Review a customer lookup tool with vague inputs and generic errors.
output: A clearer tool purpose statement tied to customer identity lookup.
Specific parameter descriptions for customer ID or email.
Actionable error guidance for not found and invalid format cases.

output_examples.input: Evaluate five calendar tools that agents often confuse.
output: A recommendation to consolidate sequential scheduling steps.
Naming changes that separate lookup, booking, and cancellation workflows.
Testing scenarios for selection accuracy and error recovery.

best_practices: Describe what the tool does, when to use it, what it accepts, and what it returns.
Keep overlapping tools separate only when their behaviors or usage contexts are clearly different.
Write errors that explain the problem, the expected format, and the next recovery action.
anti_patterns: Using vague names or descriptions that force agents to infer the tool purpose.
Creating many narrow tools for steps that agents should complete as one workflow.
Returning generic errors that do not guide retry, correction, or alternative action.

faq.question: What does this skill help me design?
faq.answer: It helps design agent-facing tools with clear names, descriptions, inputs, outputs, and error handling.

faq.question: Does it work with MCP tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for fully qualified MCP tool names and consistent namespacing.

faq.question: Can it generate tool descriptions?
faq.answer: Yes. The included Python helper provides templates and evaluation helpers for structured tool descriptions.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or call external services?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed content provides documentation and local helper scripts. No outbound network calls were found.

faq.question: When should I consolidate tools?
faq.answer: Consolidate tools when several tools represent steps in one workflow and create selection ambiguity for agents.

faq.question: How should I test a tool design?
faq.answer: Use representative agent requests, compare expected and actual tool calls, then refine descriptions based on failures.
